title: Question: Extremely long compile times on linux, but very short compile times on windows
body:
---


Author Name: **Jonathon Donaldson**
Original Redmine Message: 1522 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Windows System:
* Windows 7 64-bit
* 16GB RAM
* Intel Core i5-2400 CPU @ 3.10GHz
* GCC v4.8.3

Linux System
* Ubuntu 12.04 LTS 64-bit
* 48GB RAM
* Intel Xeon CPU W5580 @ 3.2GHz x 16
* GCC v4.6.3

I'm not sure this is necessarily related to verilator itself, but when I try to compile an identical design on Linux and Windows the linux builds always take significantly more time to complete. I'm using identical verilator/compiler command-line options for both builds (same build scripts actually). In one example, I have a design that verilates, compiles, and links in ~6min on my windows machine. But when I compile that same design on my linux system it takes almost 2 hours! Both machines are reasonably fast and I would think that my linux server system would have a leg up over my smaller windows desktop system so I'm trying to think of why the build times are so radically different.  Both machines are otherwise idle and I just leave them to work on this one task so it's not like the linux machine is tied up doing something else.

The primary difference that I see is the GCC version. Does anyone know if upgrading to GCC v4.8.3 on my linux system would make that much of a difference? The process that's consuming all the time is cc1plus. Has anyone else experienced this sort of thing before?
